insight: For the Australian team in the 2026 World Cup, hydration breaks have had a notable impact on their performance. On average, they tend to gain momentum after a break, with a slight increase in their scoring rate, as indicated by the mean change of +0.050 per minute across the two breaks. However, the numbers also suggest that when Australia is pressing before a hydration break, they tend to lose momentum, with their scoring rate decreasing by an average of 0.100 per minute, and they were unable to maintain their momentum after the break in the one instance this occurred. In contrast, when they are not pressing before a break, they experience a more significant increase in their scoring rate, averaging a gain of 0.200 per minute, which could be a strategic consideration for their coaches.
